name: Captain Nemo
description: Captain Nemo, rendered in lush watercolors, possesses a face carved with the harsh lines of the ocean's fury and the quiet contemplation of submerged worlds. His skin is a deep, weathered bronze, kissed by perpetual sun and salt spray, etched with fine wrinkles that radiate from piercing, dark eyes the color of a stormy sea. A strong, aquiline nose dominates his face, casting a shadow upon a meticulously groomed, yet subtly untamed, salt-and-pepper beard that flows almost to his chest. He is draped in a custom-tailored diving suit, a deep indigo so saturated it borders on black, accented with copper rivets and intricate, bioluminescent green circuitry that pulses faintly beneath the fabric. Over this, he often wears a long, flowing coat of waterproofed canvas, the color of aged parchment, stained with splashes of seawater and hints of verdigris from ancient shipwrecks. A broad-brimmed, diving helmet sits at his side, rendered in burnished brass and thick glass, hinting at the secrets it has witnessed in the ocean's depths. The overall color palette favors deep blues, greens, and browns, punctuated by pops of luminous green and warm copper tones, creating an image of both formidable power and melancholic beauty.
